В подкасте, в котором участвует клинический эпидемиолог Даниэла Манно, обсуждаются причины вспышки, меры по ее сдерживанию и причины особой обеспокоенности, вызванной этим заболеванием.
90fcd73b4e3f.original_description:
An outbreak of Ebola has emerged in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda, leading to nearly 400 confirmed cases and more than 100 deaths. To find out how the outbreak might have begun, what authorities can do to contain it and why this outbreak is causing particular concern, Ian Sample hears from Daniela Manno, a clinical epidemiologist at the London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine

Support the Guardian: theguardian.com/sciencepo

fa544de544a2.title: Hantavirus update, Pentagon’s UFO files, can art slow biological ageing? – podcast
fa544de544a2.summary:
Madeleine Finlay sits down with co-host and science editor Ian Sample to discuss three eyecatching stories from the week, including an update on hantavirus. Also on the agenda is the Pentagon’s release of a tranche of never before seen documents relating to UFOs, and a study looking at the link between taking part in cultural activities and slower biological ageing

Clips: News Nation

aa22384425be.title: Strange trip: why Trump is backing psychedelics – podcast
aa22384425be.summary: Президент Трамп подписал распоряжение, направленное на ускорение исследований и расширение доступа к психоделическим препаратам, таким как ибогаин, для лечения психических расстройств. В подкасте обсуждается роль Джо Рогана в этой инициативе и возможные причины поддержки психоделиков администрацией Трампа.
aa22384425be.original_description: Last month President Trump signed an executive order designed to fast track both research and access to psychedelic drugs as treatments for mental health illnesses. The most prominent in the order was ibogaine, a drug derived from the root bark of a West African shrub, that has shown some promise in relieving the long term effects of traumatic brain injury. Madeleine Finlay talks to journalist Mattha Busby about podcaster Joe Rogan’s role in the story, what else is behind the President’s inte

14f60a4ef955.title: Craig Venter obituary
14f60a4ef955.summary:
Pioneering and controversial geneticist who was one of the first to sequence the human genome, in part by using his own DNA

At the international BioVision conference in Lyon in February 2001, the geneticist Craig Venter performed a remarkable piece of scientific barnstorming. Human beings possess far fewer genes than science had ever realised, he announced. We have about 30,000, far lower than previous estimates of 100,000.

f84b50e79da1.title: MIT scientists discover amino acid that helps the gut heal itself
f84b50e79da1.summary: MIT scientists have identified cysteine — an amino acid found in foods like meat, dairy, beans, and nuts — as a potent trigger for intestinal repair. In mice, a cysteine-rich diet activated immune cells that released healing signals, helping stem cells rebuild damaged intestinal tissue after radiation exposure. Researchers say the discovery could eventually lead to new dietary therapies for cancer patients suffering from treatment-related gut damage.

afb61a2e8d41.title: Scientists discover the nutrient that can supercharge cellular energy
afb61a2e8d41.summary: Researchers discovered that leucine, a nutrient found in protein-rich foods, can supercharge mitochondria by protecting crucial energy-producing proteins inside cells. The breakthrough uncovers a powerful new link between diet and cellular energy — with possible implications for cancer and metabolic disease treatments.

217da1ce78c1.title: Common pesticide linked to hidden brain damage, scientists warn
217da1ce78c1.summary: Новое исследование показало, что распространенный инсектицид хлорпирифос, используемый в сельском хозяйстве, может вызывать скрытые повреждения мозга у детей еще до рождения.  Исследователи обнаружили связь между пренатальным воздействием этого пестицида и последующими нарушениями развития мозга и снижением двигательных навыков у детей в Нью-Йорке.  Данные результаты вызывают серьезную обеспокоенность по поводу потенциального долгосрочного влияния этого химиката на здоровье детей.
217da1ce78c1.original_description: Scientists have uncovered alarming new evidence that a common insecticide may leave lasting marks on the developing brain before a child is even born. Researchers studying New York City children found that prenatal exposure to chlorpyrifos — a pesticide once widely used indoors and still used in agriculture — was linked to widespread brain abnormalities and weaker motor skills years later.

d24c050330fe.title: Childhood junk food may rewire the brain for life
d24c050330fe.summary: Eating too much junk food early in life may rewire the brain in ways that last into adulthood, even after switching to a healthier diet. Scientists found that high-fat, high-sugar diets changed feeding behavior and disrupted appetite-control regions in the brain. Excitingly, certain gut-friendly bacteria and prebiotic fibers appeared to help undo some of the damage.

ab83c6b40bda.title: Scientists discover strange “narwhal” waves that trap light beyond known limits
ab83c6b40bda.summary: Physicists at Peking University have uncovered a new way to confine light far beyond conventional limits — without relying on metals and their inherent energy dissipation. By formulating the singular dispersion equation, the team discovered narwhal-shaped wavefunctions that trap light at deep-subwavelength volumes in purely dielectric materials. The advance, dubbed singulonics, could pave the way for ultra-efficient photonic chips, new quantum technologies, and imaging tools with unprecedented r

5a8ab940edfb.title: Physicists finally solve the strange mystery of “breathing” lasers
5a8ab940edfb.summary: Scientists have finally figured out how mysterious “breather” laser pulses work, solving a puzzle that has frustrated laser physicists for years. These unusual ultrafast lasers produce light pulses that rhythmically grow and shrink instead of staying steady, almost like they’re breathing.

4e8d62fb755b.title: Jupiter’s lightning may be 100x more powerful than Earth’s
4e8d62fb755b.summary: Jupiter’s storms aren’t just gigantic — they may unleash lightning far more powerful than anything on Earth. Using NASA’s Juno spacecraft, scientists discovered that some lightning bolts on the gas giant could pack up to 100 times the punch of Earth’s lightning, and possibly much more. The findings reveal that Jupiter’s atmosphere works very differently from our own, with massive storms building enormous amounts of energy before erupting in violent flashes across cloud tops towering more than 10

8f99ae2dc29b.title: Scientists discover a strange “inside-out” planetary system that shouldn’t exist
8f99ae2dc29b.summary: Scientists have discovered a bizarre planetary system where a rocky world orbits farther out than giant gas planets, defying long-standing theories of planet formation. The finding hints that some planets may form much later than expected — and that our Solar System might not be as typical as we thought.

The finding hints that some planets may form much later than expected — and that our Solar System might not be as typical as we thought.

b6af4039b4a6.title: Popular GLP-1 weight-loss drugs like Ozempic slash heart attack and stroke risk
b6af4039b4a6.summary: Обширный международный обзор показал, что популярные препараты для снижения веса на основе GLP-1, такие как Оземпик, значительно снижают риск сердечных приступов, инсультов, сердечной недостаточности и преждевременной смерти в долгосрочной перспективе. Исследователи считают, что эти лекарства могут стать важным инструментом в борьбе с сердечно-сосудистыми заболеваниями, а не только с ожирением и диабетом.
b6af4039b4a6.original_description: A huge international review found that GLP-1 weight-loss drugs significantly reduce the risk of heart attacks, strokes, heart failure, and premature death over the long term. Researchers say these medications could become a major weapon against cardiovascular disease — not just obesity and diabetes.

8538c39ff60f.title: Scientists discover hidden weakness shared by hundreds of cancer mutations
8538c39ff60f.summary: Scientists have unveiled a powerful new tool called PerturbFate that could change how researchers tackle diseases driven by huge numbers of genetic mutations, including cancer and Alzheimer’s. Instead of trying to target every faulty gene individually, the system tracks how different mutations reshape cells over time and identifies the hidden “control hubs” where those pathways converge.

5cb9afccb1f7.title: UNESCO warns a tsunami in the Mediterranean is inevitable
5cb9afccb1f7.summary: ЮНЕСКО предупреждает о неизбежности цунами в Средиземном море, несмотря на то, что регион считается относительно безопасным. Исторические данные и современные модели показывают, что разрушительные волны уже обрушивались на побережье, и новая опасность может возникнуть очень быстро, с минимальным временем для оповещения.
5cb9afccb1f7.original_description: The French Riviera may look like an unlikely place for a tsunami disaster, but scientists warn the threat is far more real than most people realize. Historical events and new modeling show that destructive waves have already struck the Mediterranean coast — and could hit again with very little warning. Some tsunami scenarios could reach beaches in under 10 minutes, leaving almost no time for traditional alerts.

c396152cc659.title: Scientists solve 320-million-year mystery of reptile bone armor
c396152cc659.summary: Reptiles have been growing armor in their skin on and off for hundreds of millions of years, but scientists never fully understood how it evolved. A massive new evolutionary study shows these skin bones appeared independently in multiple lizard groups rather than coming from a single armored ancestor. Even more astonishing, Australian goannas lost this armor long ago — then evolved it back again millions of years later.

8ef40e39ab5f.title: New quantum sensor could count individual photons and hunt dark matter
8ef40e39ab5f.summary: Researchers have built an ultra-sensitive sensor capable of detecting unimaginably small amounts of energy — below one zeptojoule. The breakthrough relies on fragile superconducting materials that react to even the slightest temperature change. This level of precision could improve quantum computers, enable photon counting, and even help scientists detect elusive dark matter particles from space.

4b91cbdb4367.title: Scientists found a hidden Alzheimer’s trigger and shut it down
4b91cbdb4367.summary: Ученые обнаружили фермент IDOL, который может стать новой мишенью в борьбе с болезнью Альцгеймера. Исследования показали, что подавление IDOL значительно снижает образование амилоидных бляшек и улучшает работу нейронов, что открывает перспективы для создания препаратов, способных не только замедлять прогрессирование болезни, но и защищать мозг от дальнейшего ухудшения.
4b91cbdb4367.original_description: A newly identified enzyme called IDOL could become a major new target in the fight against Alzheimer’s disease. Researchers found that removing it from neurons sharply reduced amyloid plaques and improved key brain processes linked to resilience and communication between cells. The discovery may lead to future treatments that go beyond slowing Alzheimer’s — potentially helping protect the brain from further decline.

d464eccfbef2.title: Scientists use light to create tiny molecules that could transform medicine
d464eccfbef2.summary: Researchers have developed a light-driven method for creating tiny, high-energy “housane” molecules that are valuable for drug development and materials science. These compact ring-shaped structures are difficult to produce because of the intense internal strain they contain. By using photocatalysis and carefully tuning the starting molecules, the team managed to guide the reaction into a clean and efficient pathway.

5034f052ff6d.title: Britain’s 11,000-year-old “oldest northerner” was a 3-year-old girl, DNA reveals
5034f052ff6d.summary: Новейшие исследования ДНК выявили самые древние известные человеческие останки в Северной Британии – останки трехлетней девочки, жившей около 11 000 лет назад. Обнаруженные в кумбрианской пещере, останки, получившие прозвище "Ossick Lass", свидетельствуют о глубоком духовном значении этого места для ранних охотников-собирателей Британии, что проливает свет на их жизнь и смерть после окончания ледникового периода.
5034f052ff6d.original_description: Scientists have identified the oldest known human remains in Northern Britain as a young girl who lived around 11,000 years ago. Found in a Cumbrian cave and nicknamed the “Ossick Lass,” she was likely between 2.5 and 3.5 years old when she died. Nearby jewelry and evidence of multiple burials suggest the cave held deep spiritual importance for some of Britain’s earliest hunter-gatherers. The discovery is shedding new light on life — and death — just after the Ice Age.

95792ef79793.title: Scientists found a giant magnetic “twist” hidden inside the Milky Way
95792ef79793.summary: Astronomers have uncovered a strange magnetic “flip” hidden inside the Milky Way. Using a new radio telescope, researchers mapped the galaxy’s magnetic field in unprecedented detail and discovered that a mysterious reversal in the Sagittarius Arm cuts diagonally across space. The finding could reshape how scientists understand the structure and future evolution of our galaxy.

f2223cb89869.title: Scientists use DNA from poop to save the world’s rarest marsupial
f2223cb89869.summary: Scientists in Australia are using cutting-edge DNA techniques to help save one of the world’s rarest marsupials — the critically endangered Gilbert’s potoroo, with fewer than 150 left in the wild. By analyzing tiny traces of DNA in the animals’ scat, researchers uncovered clues about the elusive fungi the potoroos depend on for survival. The findings could help conservationists identify safer new habitats and establish backup populations before disasters like bushfires wipe them out.

6a4e22310da4.title: Scientists discover massive natural hydrogen source beneath Canada
6a4e22310da4.summary: Scientists in Canada have discovered that ancient underground rocks are naturally producing hydrogen gas — and lots of it. Measurements from mine boreholes in Ontario show the gas can flow continuously for years, offering a potential new source of clean energy called “white hydrogen.” Researchers say this hidden resource could help power industries and remote communities while cutting carbon emissions and reducing dependence on fossil fuels.

7f89c347b819.title: T. rex’s tiny arms may have evolved for a surprisingly brutal reason
7f89c347b819.summary: Why did T. rex have such tiny arms? Scientists now think it’s because its giant head became the ultimate hunting tool. Across multiple dinosaur groups, stronger skulls and crushing jaws evolved alongside shrinking forelimbs, especially in predators hunting enormous prey. In other words, once the bite became deadly enough, the arms may have stopped mattering.

0b1732e33ab1.title: Mathematicians stunned by AI's biggest breakthrough in mathematics yet
0b1732e33ab1.summary: Искусственный интеллект компании OpenAI совершил прорыв в математике, решив десятилетнюю задачу, поставленную Павлом Эрдешом. Это событие названо математиками значительным шагом для развития искусственного интеллекта в этой области. Решение демонстрирует потенциал ИИ в решении сложных математических проблем, ранее считавшихся недоступными.
0b1732e33ab1.original_description: Artificial intelligence built by OpenAI has cracked a decades-old conjecture by Paul Erdős, which mathematicians have hailed as a monumental moment for AI in mathematics

32f36178bd74.title: Enhanced Games could tempt more young people into doping, Wada warns
32f36178bd74.summary: Всемирное антидопинговое агентство (WADA) выразило обеспокоенность тем, что соревнования Enhanced Games, проходящие в Лас-Вегасе, могут спровоцировать молодежь на использование допинга.  WADA считает, что данное мероприятие противоречит принципам борьбы с допингом, поскольку допускает употребление запрещенных препаратов и предлагает крупные призы за установление новых рекордов.
32f36178bd74.original_description:
• Event ‘goes against everything Wada stands for’

  • Inaugural competition is on Sunday in Las Vegas

The World Anti-Doping Agency says it is concerned that Enhanced Games’ athletes will tempt more young people into using performance-enhancing drugs.

The stark warning comes before the controversial $50m event in Las Vegas on Sunday, which allows competitors to take banned drugs – and offers huge prizes if they win races and break world records.

1892e2a9e32b.summary: Partick Thistle and St Mirren go into the second leg of their William Hill Scottish Premiership play-off final on an even keel after battling out a 1-1 draw at Firhill.

7b492a90ede3.title: Eckert accepts responsibilty for 'deplorable' Southampton spying scandal
7b492a90ede3.summary: Southampton boss Tonda Eckert has taken responsibility for a "contrived, determined and deplorable" spying operation that led to the club's expulsion from the Championship play-off final, an independent commission has said.

e05263e26469.title: Lewis scores hat-trick as Robins run riot against young Warriors ahead of Cup final
e05263e26469.summary: Mikey Lewis scored an 18-minute hat-trick as Hull KR ran riot in a 62-4 victory over an under-strength Wigan Warriors side in a dress rehearsal for next week's Challenge Cup final.
